overview: Situated on the mid-Atlantic island of Terceira, Angra was an obligatory port of call for fleets from Africa and the Indies. Its 400-year-old San Sebastião and San João Baptista fortifications are unique examples of military architecture.
history_significance: A vital maritime link between Europe and the New World during the Age of Discovery.
highlights: Sé Cathedral of Angra, Fort of São Sebastião, and the colorful Impérios.

name: Monastery of the Hieronymites and Tower of Belém in Lisbon
overview: Standing at the entrance to Lisbon harbour, the Monastery of the Hieronymites and the Tower of Belém are the most prominent examples of Portuguese architecture in the Manueline style.
history_significance: Built to commemorate Vasco da Gama's voyage and to give thanks for the Virgin Mary's protection.
highlights: The ornate Cloisters of the Monastery, Vasco da Gama's Tomb, and the Belém Tower.

name: Monastery of Batalha
overview: The Monastery of the Dominicans of Batalha was built to commemorate the victory of the Portuguese over the Castilians at the battle of Aljubarrota in 1385.
history_significance: A masterpiece of Gothic and Manueline art, symbolizing Portugal's independence.
highlights: The Unfinished Chapels (Capelas Imperfeitas) and the Founder's Chapel.

name: Convent of Christ in Tomar
overview: Originally a Templar stronghold built in the 12th century, the Convent of Christ is a historic monument that blends Romanesque, Gothic, Manueline, and Renaissance styles.
history_significance: The headquarters of the Order of Christ, which played a crucial role in the Portuguese maritime discoveries.
highlights: The Charola (Rotunda) and the famous Manueline Window.

name: Historic Centre of Évora
overview: This museum-city, whose roots go back to Roman times, reached its golden age in the 15th century when it became the residence of the Portuguese kings.
history_significance: Famous for its whitewashed houses decorated with azulejos and wrought-iron balconies.
highlights: The Roman Temple of Diana, Cathedral of Évora, and the Chapel of Bones.

name: Monastery of Alcobaça
overview: The Abbey of Santa Maria d'Alcobaça was founded in the 12th century by King Afonso I. Its size, the purity of its architectural style, and the beauty of the materials make it a masterpiece of Cistercian Gothic art.
history_significance: Contains the tombs of King Pedro I and Inês de Castro, famous for their tragic love story.
highlights: The Royal Tombs and the massive Kitchen with its river diversion.

name: Cultural Landscape of Sintra
overview: In the 19th century, Sintra became the first centre of European Romantic architecture. Ferdinand II turned a ruined monastery into a castle where this new sensitivity was displayed.
history_significance: A pioneering approach to Romantic landscaping that influenced parks and gardens across Europe.
highlights: Pena Palace, Castle of the Moors, and Quinta da Regaleira.

name: Historic Centre of Oporto, Luiz I Bridge and Monastery of Serra do Pilar
overview: Built along the hillsides overlooking the mouth of the Douro River, Oporto is an outstanding urban landscape with a 2,000-year history. It includes the famous Luiz I Bridge.
history_significance: Commercial and maritime center known for Port wine and its rich Neoclassical and Beaux-Arts architecture.
highlights: Luiz I Bridge, Sao Bento Station (azulejos), and the Clérigos Tower.

name: Laurisilva of Madeira
overview: The Laurisilva of Madeira is an outstanding relict of a previously widespread laurel forest type. It is the largest surviving area of laurel forest.
history_significance: Contains a unique suite of plants and animals, including the Madeiran long-toed pigeon.
highlights: Rabaçal (levada walks) and the Fanal forest.

name: Historic Centre of Guimarães
overview: Guimarães is associated with the emergence of the Portuguese national identity in the 12th century. It is an exceptionally well-preserved and authentic example of the evolution of a medieval settlement.
history_significance: Known as the 'Cradle of Portugal' and the birthplace of Afonso Henriques, the first king.
highlights: Guimarães Castle, Palace of the Dukes of Braganza, and Largo da Oliveira.

name: Alto Douro Wine Region
overview: Wine has been produced by traditional landholders in the Alto Douro region for some 2,000 years. The landscape is characterized by steep terraced vineyards along the Douro River.
history_significance: The world's first demarcated and regulated wine region (1756), famous for Port wine.
highlights: The terraced vineyards (socalcos) and the Quintas (wine estates).

name: Landscape of the Pico Island Vineyard Culture
overview: The site consists of a spectacular network of long, linear stone walls running inland from, and parallel to, the rocky coast. These walls protect the vines from wind and seawater.
history_significance: A unique man-made landscape adapted to volcanic terrain for wine production since the 15th century.
highlights: The black lava stone walls (currais) and the historic windmills.

name: Garrison Border Town of Elvas and its Fortifications
overview: Elvas is the largest bulwarked dry-ditch system in the world. The site includes the town's fortifications, two star-shaped forts, and the Amoreira Aqueduct.
history_significance: A masterpiece of military architecture that played a key role in Portugal's independence wars.
highlights: Fort of Graça, Fort of Santa Luzia, and the Amoreira Aqueduct.

locations.name: Fort of Nossa Senhora da Graça

name: University of Coimbra – Alta and Sofia
overview: Situated on a hill overlooking the city, the University of Coimbra grew and evolved over more than seven centuries within the old town.
history_significance: One of the oldest universities in the world (est. 1290), influencing learning and literature in the Portuguese-speaking world.
highlights: Joanina Library, Chapel of São Miguel, and the University Tower.

name: Royal Building of Mafra – Palace, Basilica, Convent, Cerco Garden and Hunting Park (Tapada)
overview: This monumental Baroque complex was built by King João V in the 18th century. It includes a Royal Palace, a Basilica, a Convent, and a vast library.
history_significance: Illustrates the power and reach of the Portuguese Empire during the gold rush period, featuring two carillons with 98 bells.
highlights: The Basilica with its six organs, the Rococo Library, and the Hunting Park.

name: Sanctuary of Bom Jesus do Monte in Braga
overview: Located on the slopes of Mount Espinho, this cultural landscape evokes the Christian Jerusalem. It features a monumental Baroque stairway that climbs 116 meters.
history_significance: A masterpiece of European Sacri Monti (Sacred Mountains), combining architecture, sculpture, and gardens.
highlights: The Baroque Stairway of the Five Senses and the church at the summit.

name: Historic Centre of Mexico City and Xochimilco
overview: Built on the ruins of Tenochtitlan, the Aztec capital, Mexico City is one of the largest and most significant cities in the world. Xochimilco preserves the Aztec tradition of chinampas (floating gardens).
history_significance: Illustrates the clash and fusion of Aztec and Spanish cultures, featuring 5 Aztec temples and the largest cathedral in the Americas.
highlights: Zócalo, Metropolitan Cathedral, Templo Mayor, and the canals of Xochimilco.

name: Pre-Hispanic City and National Park of Palenque
overview: A prime example of a Maya sanctuary of the classical period. Palenque flourished between AD 500 and 700.
history_significance: Known for the elegance and craftsmanship of its buildings and the sculpted reliefs illustrating Maya mythology.
highlights: Temple of the Inscriptions (Tomb of Pakal) and The Palace.

name: Pre-Hispanic City of Teotihuacan
overview: The holy city of Teotihuacan ('the place where the gods were created') is situated some 50 km north-east of Mexico City. It was one of the most powerful cultural centers in Mesoamerica.
history_significance: Characterized by the vast size of its monuments, in particular the Temple of Quetzalcoatl and the Pyramids of the Sun and the Moon.
highlights: Pyramid of the Sun, Pyramid of the Moon, and the Avenue of the Dead.

name: Historic Centre of Oaxaca and Archaeological Site of Monte Albán
overview: This property comprises two distinct sites: the colonial city of Oaxaca and the pre-Hispanic Zapotec capital of Monte Albán located on a flattened mountain top.
history_significance: Monte Albán was the political center of the Zapotec civilization for over 1,000 years. Oaxaca is a perfect example of a 16th-century colonial town.
highlights: Santo Domingo Church (Oaxaca) and the Grand Plaza (Monte Albán).

name: Historic Centre of Puebla
overview: Founded ex nihilo in 1531, Puebla has preserved its great religious structures such as the 16th-century cathedral and fine buildings like the old archbishop's palace.
history_significance: Famous for the fusion of European and American styles, particularly in the use of Talavera tiles (azulejos).
highlights: Puebla Cathedral, Palafoxiana Library, and the Chapel of the Rosary.

name: Sian Ka'an
overview: In the language of the Maya peoples who once inhabited this region, Sian Ka'an means 'Origin of the Sky'. It is a complex hydrological system with tropical forests, mangroves, and marshes.
history_significance: Provides a habitat for a remarkably rich flora and fauna, including over 300 species of birds and the Mesoamerican Barrier Reef.
highlights: Muyil archaeological site and the mangrove canals.

name: Historic Town of Guanajuato and Adjacent Mines
overview: Founded by the Spanish in the early 16th century, Guanajuato became the world's leading silver-extraction center in the 18th century.
history_significance: Its 'Boca del Infierno' mineshaft plunges 600m. The town is known for its subterranean streets and Baroque architecture.
highlights: Juárez Theater, the Basilica, and the Valenciana Mine.

name: Pre-Hispanic City of Chichen-Itza
overview: Chichen-Itza was one of the greatest Maya centers of the Yucatán peninsula. It exhibits a multitude of architectural styles, showing the fusion of Maya and Toltec cultures.
history_significance: Home to the Temple of Kukulcan (El Castillo), which demonstrates advanced astronomical knowledge.
highlights: El Castillo (Pyramid), Great Ball Court, and the Temple of the Warriors.

name: Historic Centre of Morelia
overview: Built in the 16th century, Morelia is an outstanding example of urban planning which combines the ideas of the Spanish Renaissance with the Mesoamerican experience.
history_significance: Its streets are lined with more than 200 historic buildings built from the region's characteristic pink stone.
highlights: Morelia Cathedral and the Aqueduct.

name: El Tajin, Pre-Hispanic City
overview: Located in the state of Veracruz, El Tajin was the capital of the Totonac culture. It flourished from the early 9th to the early 13th century.
history_significance: Famous for the Pyramid of the Niches, a masterpiece of ancient Mexican and American architecture.
highlights: Pyramid of the Niches and the Great Xicalcoliuhqui.

name: Whale Sanctuary of El Vizcaino
overview: Located in the central part of the peninsula of Baja California, the sanctuary contains some of the world's most important reproduction sites for the grey whale.
history_significance: Also a refuge for harbor seals, California sea lions, and the endangered pronghorn.
highlights: Ojo de Liebre Lagoon and San Ignacio Lagoon.

name: Historic Centre of Zacatecas
overview: Founded in 1546 after the discovery of a rich silver lode, Zacatecas reached the height of its prosperity in the 16th and 17th centuries.
history_significance: Notable for its cathedral with a highly decorated facade, representing one of the finest examples of Churrigueresque architecture.
highlights: Zacatecas Cathedral and the Mina El Edén.

name: Rock Paintings of the Sierra de San Francisco
overview: The Sierra de San Francisco represents one of the most outstanding concentrations of prehistoric art in the world.
history_significance: The 'Great Murals' depict human figures and animals in red, black, white, and yellow, dating from c. 100 B.C. to A.D. 1300.
highlights: Cueva del Ratón and Cueva Pintada.

name: Earliest 16th-Century Monasteries on the Slopes of Popocatepetl
overview: These 14 monasteries were built by the Franciscans, Dominicans, and Augustinians in the early 16th century to evangelize the indigenous populations.
history_significance: They served as the model for monastery and church architecture throughout New Spain.
highlights: San Miguel Huejotzingo, San Andrés Calpan, and Tepoztlán.

name: Historic Monuments Zone of Querétaro
overview: The old colonial town of Querétaro is unusual in having retained the geometric street plan of the Spanish conquerors side by side with the twisting alleys of the Indian quarters.
history_significance: Famous for its 74-arched aqueduct and the harmonious integration of different urban layouts.
highlights: The Aqueduct, Temple of Santa Rosa de Viterbo, and Plaza de Armas.

name: Pre-Hispanic Town of Uxmal
overview: Uxmal is one of the most magnificent Maya cities in Yucatán, founded c. A.D. 700. It represents the pinnacle of the Puuc architectural style.
history_significance: Famous for the Pyramid of the Magician and the Governor's Palace, decorated with thousands of mosaic elements.
highlights: Pyramid of the Magician and the Nunnery Quadrangle.

name: Hospicio Cabañas, Guadalajara
overview: Built at the beginning of the 19th century to provide care and shelter for the disadvantaged. It was designed by Manuel Tolsá.
history_significance: Known for the harmonious relationship between its open spaces and buildings, and for the murals painted by José Clemente Orozco.
highlights: The Chapel featuring Orozco's 'Man of Fire' mural.

name: Archaeological Zone of Paquimé, Casas Grandes
overview: Paquimé played a key role in trade and cultural contacts between the Pueblo culture of the south-western US and the civilizations of Mesoamerica.
history_significance: Characterized by its adobe architecture, T-shaped doorways, and complex water control system.
highlights: The labyrinthine adobe apartments and ceremonial mounds.

name: Historic Monuments Zone of Tlacotalpan
overview: An exceptionally well-preserved Spanish colonial river port on the Papaloapan River. It preserves a traditional townscape of wide streets and colorful houses.
history_significance: Represents the fusion of Spanish and Caribbean traditions.
highlights: The colorful colonnaded houses and the Church of La Candelaria.

name: Archaeological Monuments Zone of Xochicalco
overview: Xochicalco is an exceptionally well-preserved example of a fortified political, religious, and commercial center from the Epiclassic period (AD 650–900).
history_significance: The Temple of the Feathered Serpent features fine reliefs suggesting contacts with Maya and Toltec regions.
highlights: Temple of the Feathered Serpent and the Astronomical Observatory.

name: Historic Fortified Town of Campeche
overview: Campeche is a typical example of a harbor town from the Spanish colonial period. It kept its outer walls and system of fortifications, designed to defend against pirate attacks.
history_significance: One of the few remaining walled cities in the Americas, showing 17th and 18th-century military engineering.
highlights: The Sea Gate (Puerta de Mar) and the Baluarts.

name: Ancient Maya City and Protected Tropical Forests of Calakmul, Campeche
overview: Calakmul is a major Maya site set deep in the tropical forest of the Tierras Bajas. It played a key role in the history of this region for more than twelve centuries.
history_significance: The largest city of the Maya Classic period and a biodiversity hotspot with jaguar and puma populations.
highlights: Structure I and Structure II (massive pyramids) and the surrounding biosphere.

name: Franciscan Missions in the Sierra Gorda of Querétaro
overview: The five Franciscan missions in the Sierra Gorda were built during the last phase of the conversion to Christianity of the interior of Mexico in the mid-18th century.
history_significance: Known for their 'Mestizo Baroque' style, blending Catholic theology with indigenous symbols.
highlights: Mission of Santiago de Jalpan and Mission of Santa María del Agua de Landa.

locations.name: Santa María del Agua de Landa

name: Luis Barragán House and Studio
overview: Built in 1948 in the suburbs of Mexico City, this house represents an outstanding example of the architect's creative work in the post-Second World War period.
history_significance: The only individual property in Latin America inscribed for its modern architecture, influencing contemporary design.
highlights: The use of light, color, and space in the studio and garden.

name: Islands and Protected Areas of the Gulf of California
overview: The site comprises 244 islands, islets, and coastal areas. It has been described by Jacques Cousteau as the 'aquarium of the world'.
history_significance: Contains a striking natural beauty and is a laboratory for the investigation of speciation.
highlights: Espiritu Santo Island and the rich marine life.

name: Agave Landscape and Ancient Industrial Facilities of Tequila
overview: Between the foothills of the Tequila Volcano and the deep valley of the Rio Grande River lies an extensive landscape of blue agave, used to produce tequila.
history_significance: Illustrates the fusion of pre-Hispanic traditions of fermenting mescal juice with European distillation processes.
highlights: The agave fields and the historic distilleries in Tequila town.

name: Central University City Campus of the National Autonomous University of Mexico (UNAM)
overview: The campus constitutes a unique example of 20th-century modernism integrating urbanism, architecture, engineering, landscape design, and fine arts.
history_significance: Created by over 60 architects, engineers, and artists, including Juan O'Gorman and Diego Rivera.
highlights: Central Library (with its murals) and the Olympic Stadium.

name: Monarch Butterfly Biosphere Reserve
overview: Every autumn, millions of monarch butterflies from wide areas of North America return to this small area to overwinter in the oyamel fir forests.
history_significance: An outstanding biological phenomenon where millions of butterflies cluster on trees, turning them orange.
highlights: El Rosario Sanctuary and Sierra Chincua.

name: Protective town of San Miguel de Allende and the Sanctuary of Jesús Nazareno de Atotonilco
overview: San Miguel de Allende is an early example of a rational territorial and urban development in the 16th century. The Sanctuary of Atotonilco is known as the 'Sistine Chapel of Mexico'.
history_significance: A melting pot of Spanish, Creole, and Amerindian cultures, reflected in its Baroque and Neoclassical architecture.
highlights: The Parish Church (Parroquia) and the murals of Atotonilco.

name: Camino Real de Tierra Adentro
overview: Also known as the Silver Route, this property consists of 55 sites along a 1,400 km section of the route that linked Mexico City with Santa Fe.
history_significance: Used for 300 years to transport silver from the mines of Zacatecas, Guanajuato, and San Luis Potosí, and to facilitate trade.
highlights: Historic centers of Querétaro, San Luis Potosí, and the Bridge of Ojuelos.

name: Prehistoric Caves of Yagul and Mitla in the Central Valley of Oaxaca
overview: This property comprises two pre-Hispanic archaeological complexes and a series of prehistoric caves and rock shelters.
history_significance: Contains 10,000-year-old seeds (Cucurbitaceae) and the earliest evidence of the domestication of maize (corn).
highlights: Guilá Naquitz Cave and the Yagul archaeological site.

name: El Pinacate and Gran Desierto de Altar Biosphere Reserve
overview: The site features a dramatic landscape of active sand dunes and a dormant volcanic shield with 10 massive maar craters.
history_significance: Home to the spectacular Maar craters like El Elegante and the active Gran Altar dunes.
highlights: El Elegante Crater and the Star Dunes.

name: Aqueduct of Padre Tembleque Hydraulic System
overview: This 16th-century aqueduct stretches 45 km. It was initiated by the Franciscan friar Padre Tembleque to bring water to indigenous communities.
history_significance: A masterpiece of hydraulic engineering, featuring the highest single-level arcade ever built in an aqueduct.
highlights: The Main Arcade (Arquería Mayor) crossing the Papalote ravine.

name: Archipiélago de Revillagigedo
overview: Located in the eastern Pacific Ocean, this archipelago consists of four submerged volcanic islands. It is a critical habitat for sharks and rays.
history_significance: Known as the 'Galapagos of Mexico', it is a key site for the study of island biogeography and marine megafauna.
highlights: Socorro Island and the abundance of giant manta rays.

name: Tehuacán-Cuicatlán Valley: originary habitat of Mesoamerica
overview: The valley is part of the Mesoamerican region. It is the most biodiverse arid zone in North America and holds evidence of the domestication of plants.
history_significance: Contains one of the densest forests of columnar cacti in the world and archaeological evidence of early agriculture.
highlights: The columnar cacti forests and the Helia Bravo Hollis Botanical Garden.

name: Wixárika Route through Sacred Sites to Wirikuta (Tatehuarí Huajuyé)
overview: This cultural route traverses approximately 800 km from the Pacific coast (Nayarit) to the sacred desert of Wirikuta (San Luis Potosí). It is the foundation of the cosmology and identity of the Wixárika (Huichol) people.
history_significance: Represents one of the few remaining indigenous pilgrimage routes in the Americas maintained with its original structure and function. It embodies the connection between the people, the ancestors, and nature.
highlights: Real de Catorce, the Cerro Quemado (Birthplace of the Sun), and the sacred peyote rituals in the desert.

name: Agra Fort
overview: Near the gardens of the Taj Mahal stands the important 16th-century Mughal monument known as the Red Fort of Agra. It comprises many fairy-tale palaces, such as the Jahangir Palace and the Khas Mahal.
history_significance: The main residence of the emperors of the Mughal Dynasty until 1638, witnessing the zenith of Mughal power.
highlights: Diwan-i-Am, Diwan-i-Khas, and the view of the Taj Mahal from the Musamman Burj.

name: Ajanta Caves
overview: The first Buddhist cave monuments at Ajanta date from the 2nd and 1st centuries B.C. They contain paintings and sculptures considered to be masterpieces of Buddhist religious art.
history_significance: These caves preserve some of the finest surviving examples of ancient Indian art, particularly expressive paintings.
highlights: Cave 1 (Bodhisattva Padmapani painting) and the Chaitya halls.

name: Ellora Caves
overview: These 34 monasteries and temples, extending over more than 2 km, were dug side by side in the wall of a high basalt cliff. They represent Buddhist, Hindu, and Jain faiths.
history_significance: A monument to the spirit of tolerance that was characteristic of ancient India, built between the 6th and 10th centuries.
highlights: Kailasa Temple (Cave 16), a massive monolithic excavation.

name: Taj Mahal
overview: An immense mausoleum of white marble, built in Agra between 1631 and 1648 by order of the Mughal emperor Shah Jahan in memory of his favourite wife, Mumtaz Mahal.
history_significance: The jewel of Muslim art in India and one of the universally admired masterpieces of the world's heritage.
highlights: The white marble dome, the Pietra Dura inlay work, and the Charbagh gardens.

name: Group of Monuments at Mahabalipuram
overview: This group of sanctuaries, founded by the Pallava kings, was carved out of rock along the Coromandel coast in the 7th and 8th centuries.
history_significance: Famous for its rathas (temples in the form of chariots), mandapas (cave sanctuaries), and giant open-air reliefs.
highlights: Shore Temple, Descent of the Ganges (Arjuna's Penance), and the Five Rathas.

name: Sun Temple, Konârak
overview: On the shores of the Bay of Bengal, the temple serves as a monumental representation of the sun god Surya's chariot; its 24 wheels are decorated with symbolic designs.
history_significance: Built in the 13th century, it is one of the most famous Brahman sanctuaries in India.
highlights: The intricately carved stone wheels and the erotic sculptures.

name: Kaziranga National Park
overview: In the heart of Assam, this park is one of the last areas in eastern India undisturbed by a human presence. It is inhabited by the world's largest population of one-horned rhinoceroses.
history_significance: A major success story in the conservation of the Indian Rhinoceros.
highlights: One-horned Rhinoceros sightings and the Brahmaputra river floodplain.

name: Keoladeo National Park
overview: This former duck-hunting reserve of the Maharajas is one of the major wintering areas for large numbers of aquatic birds from Afghanistan, Turkmenistan, China, and Siberia.
history_significance: The only known wintering ground of the critically endangered Siberian Crane in the region (historically).
highlights: Bird watching, cycling through the park, and the Painted Storks.

name: Manas Wildlife Sanctuary
overview: Situated on a gentle slope in the foothills of the Himalayas, Manas contains a great variety of wildlife, including many endangered species.
history_significance: Home to rare species like the tiger, pygmy hog, Indian rhinoceros, and Indian elephant.
highlights: Project Tiger reserve and the scenic Manas River.

name: Churches and Convents of Goa
overview: The churches and convents of Goa, the former capital of the Portuguese Indies, illustrate the evangelization of Asia.
history_significance: The Basilica of Bom Jesus holds the tomb of St Francis Xavier and exemplifies the spread of Manueline, Mannerist, and Baroque art in Asia.
highlights: Basilica of Bom Jesus and Se Cathedral.

name: Fatehpur Sikri
overview: Built during the second half of the 16th century by the Emperor Akbar, Fatehpur Sikri (the City of Victory) was the capital of the Mughal Empire for only some 10 years.
history_significance: A unique architectural ensemble blending Islamic and Hindu elements, abandoned due to water scarcity.
highlights: Buland Darwaza (Gate of Magnificence), Tomb of Salim Chishti, and Panch Mahal.

name: Group of Monuments at Hampi
overview: The austere, grandiose site of Hampi was the last capital of the last great Hindu Kingdom of Vijayanagar. Its fabulously rich princes built Dravidian temples and palaces.
history_significance: Represents the peak of the Vijayanagara Empire (14th-16th century) before its destruction by the Deccan sultanates.
highlights: Virupaksha Temple, Vittala Temple (Stone Chariot), and the Elephant Stables.

name: Khajuraho Group of Monuments
overview: The temples at Khajuraho were built during the Chandella dynasty, which reached its apogee between 950 and 1050. Only about 20 temples have survived.
history_significance: Famous for their nagara-style architectural symbolism and their erotic sculptures.
highlights: Kandariya Mahadeva Temple and Lakshmana Temple.

name: Elephanta Caves
overview: The 'City of Caves', on an island in the Sea of Oman close to Mumbai, contains a collection of rock art linked to the cult of Shiva.
history_significance: The main cave (Great Cave 1) contains the colossal Trimurti sculpture representing the three aspects of Shiva.
highlights: The Trimurti (three-headed Shiva) and the ferry ride from Gateway of India.

name: Great Living Chola Temples
overview: These temples were built by kings of the Chola Empire, which stretched over all of south India and the neighbouring islands. The site includes three great 11th- and 12th-century Temples.
history_significance: Testifies to the brilliant achievements of the Chola in architecture, sculpture, painting, and bronze casting.
highlights: Brihadisvara Temple (Thanjavur), Airavatesvara Temple, and their massive vimanas.

name: Group of Monuments at Pattadakal
overview: Pattadakal represents the high point of an eclectic art which, in the 7th and 8th centuries under the Chalukya dynasty, achieved a harmonious blend of architectural forms from northern and southern India.
history_significance: The site of royal coronations, featuring an impressive series of nine Hindu temples and a Jain sanctuary.
highlights: Virupaksha Temple and Mallikarjuna Temple.

name: Sundarbans National Park
overview: The Sundarbans covers 10,000 sq. km of land and water (shared with Bangladesh) in the Ganges delta. It contains the world's largest area of mangrove forests.
history_significance: One of the largest reserves for the Bengal tiger and home to the estuarine crocodile.
highlights: The mangrove ecosystem and the Royal Bengal Tiger.

name: Nanda Devi and Valley of Flowers National Parks
overview: Nestled high in the West Himalayas, the Valley of Flowers is renowned for its meadows of endemic alpine flowers. Nanda Devi National Park is dominated by the 7,816m peak of Nanda Devi.
history_significance: A habitat for rare and endangered animals, including the Asiatic black bear, snow leopard, and blue sheep.
highlights: The floral blooms (July-August) and the rugged mountain wilderness.

name: Buddhist Monuments at Sanchi
overview: On a hill overlooking the plain, Sanchi comprises a group of Buddhist monuments (monolithic pillars, palaces, temples, and monasteries) dating from the 2nd and 1st centuries B.C. to the 12th century A.D.
history_significance: The oldest Buddhist sanctuary in existence and a major center of Buddhism in India until the 12th century.
highlights: The Great Stupa (Stupa 1) with its four ornamental gateways (Toranas).

name: Humayun's Tomb, Delhi
overview: Built in 1570, this tomb is of particular cultural significance as it was the first garden-tomb on the Indian subcontinent.
history_significance: Its unique architecture and Persian influence inspired several major architectural innovations, culminating in the construction of the Taj Mahal.
highlights: The double dome and the Charbagh (four-quadrant) garden.

name: Qutb Minar and its Monuments, Delhi
overview: Built in the early 13th century, the red sandstone tower of Qutb Minar is 72.5 m high. The surrounding archaeological area contains funerary buildings and two mosques.
history_significance: Represents the artistic vision of the early Islamic rulers of India (Mamluk Dynasty).
highlights: Qutb Minar, the Iron Pillar, and the Alai Darwaza.

name: Mountain Railways of India
overview: This site includes three railways: the Darjeeling Himalayan Railway, the Nilgiri Mountain Railway, and the Kalka Shimla Railway. They remain fully operational examples of ingenious engineering.
history_significance: Outstanding examples of bold, ingenious engineering solutions for the problem of establishing an effective rail link through rugged, mountainous terrain.
highlights: The 'Toy Trains', loops, and Z-reverses.

name: Mahabodhi Temple Complex at Bodh Gaya
overview: The Mahabodhi Temple Complex is one of the four holy sites related to the life of the Lord Buddha, and particularly to the attainment of Enlightenment.
history_significance: Contains the Bodhi Tree under which Siddhartha Gautama achieved enlightenment to become the Buddha.
highlights: The main temple tower, the Bodhi Tree, and the Vajrasana.

name: Rock Shelters of Bhimbetka
overview: These rock shelters are located in the foothills of the Vindhyan Mountains. They exhibit the earliest traces of human life on the Indian subcontinent.
history_significance: Features rock paintings dating from the Mesolithic period to the historic period, showing a continuity of cultural tradition.
highlights: Auditorium Rock and Zoo Rock paintings.

name: Champaner-Pavagadh Archaeological Park
overview: A concentration of largely unexcavated archaeological, historic, and living cultural heritage properties cradled in an impressive landscape.
history_significance: The only complete and unchanged Islamic pre-Mughal city.
highlights: Kalikamata Temple on Pavagadh Hill and the Jami Masjid.

name: Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus (formerly Victoria Terminus)
overview: This historic railway station in Mumbai is an outstanding example of Victorian Gothic Revival architecture, blended with themes from Indian traditional architecture.
history_significance: Symbolizes the meeting of two cultures (British and Indian) and the expansion of Bombay as a major mercantile port.
highlights: The central dome, the turrets, and the stained glass windows.

name: Red Fort Complex
overview: The Red Fort (Lal Qila) was built as the palace fort of Shahjahanabad, the new capital of the fifth Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan.
history_significance: Represents the zenith of Mughal creativity and was the site of Indian independence events.
highlights: Lahori Gate, Diwan-i-Am, and the Stream of Paradise (Nahr-i-Bihisht).

name: The Jantar Mantar, Jaipur
overview: The Jantar Mantar is an astronomical observation site built in the early 18th century. It includes a set of some 20 main fixed instruments.
history_significance: The most significant and best-preserved set of fixed monumental instruments in India.
highlights: The Samrat Yantra (world's largest stone sundial).

name: Western Ghats
overview: Older than the Himalayas, the mountain chain of the Western Ghats represents geomorphic features of immense importance with unique biophysical and ecological processes. (Comprises 39 distinct clusters)
history_significance: One of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity, home to the Asian Elephant and Tiger.
highlights: Eravikulam National Park (Nilgiri Tahr), Periyar Tiger Reserve, and Agasthyamalai.

name: Hill Forts of Rajasthan
overview: The serial site, situated in the state of Rajasthan, includes six majestic forts. They enclose major urban centers, palaces, trading centers, and other buildings.
history_significance: Reflects the power of the Rajput princely states that flourished from the 8th to the 18th centuries.
highlights: Amber Fort, Chittorgarh Fort, and Jaisalmer Fort.

name: Rani-ki-Vav (the Queen's Stepwell) at Patan
overview: Rani-ki-Vav is an exceptional example of a distinctive form of subterranean water resource and storage system on the Indian subcontinent.
history_significance: Built in the 11th century, it highlights the sanctity of water as an inverted temple, with over 500 principle sculptures.
highlights: The intricate sculptures of Vishnu's avatars and the geometric complexity.

name: Great Himalayan National Park Conservation Area
overview: Located in the western part of the Himalayan Mountains, this park is characterized by high alpine peaks, alpine meadows, and riverine forests.
history_significance: Protects the monsoon-affected forests and alpine meadows of the front ranges of the Himalayas.
highlights: Snow Leopards, Blue Sheep, and the Sainj/Tirthan valleys.

name: Archaeological Site of Nalanda Mahavihara at Nalanda
overview: Nalanda stands out as the most ancient university of the Indian Subcontinent. It engaged in the organized transmission of knowledge over an uninterrupted period of 800 years.
history_significance: A major Buddhist center of learning from the 5th to 12th centuries, attracting scholars from across Asia.
highlights: The stupas, shrines, and viharas (residential and educational buildings).

name: Khangchendzonga National Park
overview: Located in the heart of the Himalayan range, this park includes a unique diversity of plains, valleys, lakes, glaciers, and spectacular, snow-capped mountains covered with ancient forests.
history_significance: Home to Mount Khangchendzonga (3rd highest in the world), revered by the indigenous people of Sikkim.
highlights: Mount Khangchendzonga and the Zemu Glacier.

name: Historic City of Ahmadabad
overview: The walled city of Ahmadabad, founded by Sultan Ahmad Shah in the 15th century, presents a rich architectural heritage from the Sultanate period.
history_significance: Renowned for its 'pol' (traditional residential clusters) and the harmonious coexistence of Hindu, Islamic, and Jain communities.
highlights: Bhadra Fort, Jama Masjid, and the pols.

name: Victorian Gothic and Art Deco Ensembles of Mumbai
overview: Having become a global trading center, the city of Mumbai implemented an ambitious urban planning project in the second half of the 19th century.
history_significance: A unique blend of Indian elements with Victorian Gothic and later Art Deco styles.
highlights: The High Court, University of Mumbai buildings, and the Oval Maidan.

name: Jaipur City, Rajasthan
overview: The fortified city of Jaipur was founded in 1727. Unlike other cities in the region, it was established on a plain and built according to a grid plan.
history_significance: Designed based on ancient Hindu architectural principles (Vastu Shastra), known as the 'Pink City'.
highlights: Hawa Mahal, City Palace, and the grid street layout.

name: Kakatiya Rudreswara (Ramappa) Temple, Telangana
overview: Rudreshwara, popularly known as Ramappa Temple, is located in the village of Palampet. It is the main Shiva temple in a walled complex built during the Kakatiya period (1213 AD).
history_significance: Famous for its floating bricks and intricate carvings that demonstrate the artistic mastery of the Kakatiya dynasty.
highlights: The sandbox technology foundation and the exquisitely carved pillars.

name: Dholavira: a Harappan City
overview: The ancient city of Dholavira is one of the most remarkable and well-preserved urban settlements in South Asia dating from the 3rd to mid-2nd millennium BCE.
history_significance: A major hub of the Indus Valley Civilization, noted for its sophisticated water management system.
highlights: The massive reservoirs, the signboards, and the Citadel.

name: Santiniketan
overview: Established by Nobel laureate Rabindranath Tagore, Santiniketan was a residential school and center for art based on ancient Indian traditions and a vision of the unity of humanity.
history_significance: A distinct architectural and educational movement that diverged from British colonial and European modernism.
highlights: The Uttarayan complex, Visva-Bharati University buildings, and the glass temple.

name: Sacred Ensembles of the Hoysalas
overview: This property encompasses the three most representative examples of Hoysala temple complexes in southern India, dating from the 12th to 13th centuries.
history_significance: Renowned for their star-shaped plans and hyper-realistic stone sculptures.
highlights: Chennakeshava Temple (Belur) and Hoysaleswara Temple (Halebidu).

name: Moidams – the Mound-Burial System of the Ahom Dynasty
overview: Set in the foothills of the Patkai Ranges, the Moidams are the necropolis of the Tai-Ahom kings. These vaulted chambers (chow-chali) are covered by earthen mounds.
history_significance: The most significant physical remnant of the Ahom Dynasty, which ruled Assam for 600 years, illustrating their unique funerary traditions.
highlights: The Charaideo Maidam complex and the octagonal architecture.

name: Maratha Military Landscapes of India
overview: A serial property comprising 12 forts distributed across the diverse geographical regions of Maharashtra and Tamil Nadu. These forts demonstrate the extraordinary military fortification system developed by the Maratha rulers between the 17th and 19th centuries.
history_significance: Represents the strategic military power of the Maratha Empire, integrating unique terrain features like the Sahyadri mountains, Konkan coast, and Deccan plateau into defensive architecture.
highlights: Raigad Fort (Capital), Shivneri Fort, and the coastal Sindhudurg Fort.

name: Rock Drawings in Valcamonica
overview: Valcamonica, situated in the Lombardy plain, has one of the world's greatest collections of prehistoric petroglyphs – more than 140,000 symbols and figures carved in the rock over a period of 8,000 years.
history_significance: Illustrates the evolution of prehistoric life, agriculture, navigation, and war from the Epipalaeolithic to the Middle Ages.
highlights: Naquane National Park of Rock Engravings and the 'Camunian Rose' symbol.

locations.name: Naquane National Park of Rock Engravings
National Archaeological Park of Massi di Cemmo

name: Historic Centre of Rome, the Properties of the Holy See in that City Enjoying Extraterritorial Rights and San Paolo Fuori le Mura
overview: Founded according to legend in 753 BC, Rome was the capital of the Roman Republic and Empire, and later the centre of the Christian world.
history_significance: A matchless artistic and architectural achievement, housing monuments like the Colosseum, Pantheon, and the Roman Forum.
highlights: Colosseum, Roman Forum, Pantheon, and Basilica of San Paolo Fuori le Mura.

name: Church and Dominican Convent of Santa Maria delle Grazie with 'The Last Supper' by Leonardo da Vinci
overview: The refectory of the convent forms an integral part of this architectural complex, adorned by Leonardo da Vinci's 'The Last Supper', painted between 1495 and 1497.
history_significance: Contains one of the most famous paintings in the world, marking a new era in art history.
highlights: The Last Supper mural and the Bramante sacristy.

name: Historic Centre of Florence
overview: Built on the site of an Etruscan settlement, Florence represents the symbol of the Renaissance. Under the Medicis, it became a leading economic and cultural center.
history_significance: Home to the world's greatest concentration of universally renowned works of art, including the Duomo, Uffizi, and Pitti Palace.
highlights: Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore (Duomo), Ponte Vecchio, and Uffizi Gallery.

locations.name: Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore

name: Venice and its Lagoon
overview: Founded in the 5th century and spread over 118 small islands, Venice became a major maritime power in the 10th century. The whole city is an extraordinary architectural masterpiece.
history_significance: A unique cultural achievement where even the smallest building contains works by some of the world's greatest artists such as Giorgione, Titian, and Tintoretto.
highlights: St Mark's Basilica, Doge's Palace, and the Grand Canal.

name: Piazza del Duomo, Pisa
overview: Standing in a large green expanse, the Piazza del Duomo houses a group of monuments known the world over.
history_significance: These four masterpieces of medieval architecture – the cathedral, the baptistry, the campanile (Leaning Tower), and the cemetery – had a great influence on monumental art in Italy.
highlights: Leaning Tower of Pisa, the Cathedral, and the Baptistery.

name: Historic Centre of San Gimignano
overview: San Gimignano bears exceptional testimony to medieval civilization in Tuscany. It is famous for its tower houses, which were built by feuding noble families.
history_significance: Preserves the feudal atmosphere and urban structure of the 13th and 14th centuries.
highlights: The 14 surviving medieval towers and the Piazza della Cisterna.

name: The Sassi and the Park of the Rupestrian Churches of Matera
overview: This represents the most outstanding, intact example of a troglodyte settlement in the Mediterranean region, perfectly adapted to its terrain and ecosystem.
history_significance: Continuously inhabited from the Palaeolithic period to the modern day, showing the evolution of human settlement.
highlights: Sasso Caveoso, Sasso Barisano, and the rock-hewn churches.

name: City of Vicenza and the Palladian Villas of the Veneto
overview: Founded in the 2nd century B.C., Vicenza prospered under Venetian rule. The work of Andrea Palladio (1508–80) gives the city its unique appearance.
history_significance: Palladio's urban buildings and villas had a decisive influence on the development of architecture worldwide (Palladianism).
highlights: Teatro Olimpico, Villa La Rotonda, and Basilica Palladiana.

name: Historic Centre of Siena
overview: Siena is the embodiment of a medieval city. Its inhabitants pursued their rivalry with Florence right into the area of urban planning.
history_significance: Preserves its 12th to 15th-century Gothic appearance and the unique fan-shaped Piazza del Campo.
highlights: Piazza del Campo, Siena Cathedral, and the Palazzo Pubblico.

name: Historic Centre of Naples
overview: From the Neapolis founded by Greek settlers in 470 B.C. to the city of today, Naples has retained the imprint of the successive cultures that emerged in Europe and the Mediterranean basin.
history_significance: A wealth of historical monuments including the Church of Santa Chiara and the Castel Nuovo.
highlights: Spaccanapoli, Castel dell'Ovo, and the Royal Palace.

name: Crespi d'Adda
overview: Crespi d'Adda is an outstanding example of the 19th- and early 20th-century 'company towns' built in Europe and North America by enlightened industrialists.
history_significance: A preserved industrial village built for the workers of the Crespi cotton mill, reflecting a paternalistic vision.
highlights: The cotton mill, the workers' houses, and the cemetery mausoleum.

name: Ferrara, City of the Renaissance, and its Po Delta
overview: Ferrara grew up around a ford over the River Po and became an intellectual and artistic center. The 'Addizione Erculea' is a masterpiece of Renaissance urban planning.
history_significance: The first example of a Renaissance planned city, influencing urban design in Europe.
highlights: Castello Estense, Palazzo dei Diamanti, and the Po Delta landscape.

name: Castel del Monte
overview: Built by Emperor Frederick II in the 13th century, Castel del Monte is a unique piece of medieval military architecture with a perfect octagonal shape.
history_significance: A successful blend of elements from classical antiquity, the Islamic Orient, and north European Cistercian Gothic.
highlights: The octagonal towers and the courtyard.

name: The Trulli of Alberobello
overview: The Trulli are limestone dwellings found in the southern region of Puglia. They are remarkable examples of drywall (mortarless) construction.
history_significance: A unique prehistoric building technique still in use, characterized by pyramidal, domed, or conical roofs.
highlights: Rione Monti and the Trullo Sovrano.

name: Early Christian Monuments of Ravenna
overview: Ravenna was the seat of the Roman Empire in the 5th century and then of Byzantine Italy. It possesses a unique collection of early Christian mosaics and monuments.
history_significance: Contains the finest examples of mosaic art in Europe and evidences the fusion of western and oriental artistic styles.
highlights: Basilica of San Vitale, Mausoleum of Galla Placidia, and Basilica of Sant'Apollinare in Classe.

name: Historic Centre of the City of Pienza
overview: It was in this Tuscan town that Renaissance urban planning concepts were first put into practice. Pope Pius II decided in 1459 to transform his birthplace into an ideal Renaissance town.
history_significance: The first application of the Humanist concept of urban design.
highlights: Piazza Pio II, Palazzo Piccolomini, and the Cathedral.

name: 18th-Century Royal Palace at Caserta with the Park, the Aqueduct of Vanvitelli, and the San Leucio Complex
overview: Created by the Bourbon King Charles III in the mid-18th century, the monumental complex at Caserta was designed to rival Versailles and Madrid.
history_significance: A masterpiece of the Baroque period, integrating the palace, park, and aqueduct into a stunning landscape.
highlights: The Royal Palace, the Grand Cascade, and the English Garden.

name: Residences of the Royal House of Savoy
overview: A comprehensive overview of 17th- and 18th-century European monumental architecture, built by the Dukes of Savoy. It includes palaces in Turin and surrounding hunting lodges.
history_significance: Demonstrates the power of the absolute monarchy through a 'Zone of Command' in Turin and a 'Crown of Delights' in the countryside.
highlights: Royal Palace of Turin, Palazzina di Caccia of Stupinigi, and Palace of Venaria.

name: Botanical Garden (Orto Botanico), Padua
overview: The world's first botanical garden, created in 1545. It still preserves its original layout – a circular central plot, symbolizing the world, surrounded by a ring of water.
history_significance: The birthplace of modern botany and scientific exchange.
highlights: The ancient circular wall, the Goethe Palm, and the biodiversity garden.

name: Portovenere, Cinque Terre, and the Islands (Palmaria, Tino and Tinetto)
overview: The Ligurian coast between Cinque Terre and Portovenere is a cultural landscape of great scenic and cultural value. The layout of the small towns and the shaping of the surrounding landscape, overcoming the disadvantages of a steep, uneven terrain.
history_significance: An example of how people have adapted to a difficult natural environment over a millennium.
highlights: Manarola, Vernazza, Riomaggiore, and the terraces.

name: Cathedral, Torre Civica and Piazza Grande, Modena
overview: The magnificent 12th-century cathedral at Modena is a supreme example of early Romanesque art. It was the work of two great artists, Lanfranco and Wiligelmo.
history_significance: A masterpiece of human creative genius in the Romanesque style.
highlights: The Cathedral, the Ghirlandina Tower, and Piazza Grande.

name: Archaeological Areas of Pompei, Herculaneum and Torre Annunziata
overview: When Vesuvius erupted in AD 79, it engulfed the two flourishing Roman towns of Pompei and Herculaneum, preserving them in time.
history_significance: Provides a complete and vivid picture of society and daily life at a specific moment in the past that is without parallel anywhere in the world.
highlights: The Forum of Pompei, Villa of the Mysteries, and the Villa Oplontis.

name: Costiera Amalfitana
overview: The Amalfi Coast is an area of great physical beauty and natural diversity. It has been intensively settled by human communities since the early Middle Ages.
history_significance: A unique Mediterranean landscape with vineyards, orchards, and pastures on terraces.
highlights: Positano, Amalfi Cathedral, and Ravello (Villa Rufolo).

name: Archaeological Area of Agrigento
overview: Founded as a Greek colony in the 6th century B.C., Agrigento became one of the leading cities in the Mediterranean world. Its Doric temples are among the best preserved.
history_significance: The Valley of the Temples is a magnificent testimony to Greek civilization in Sicily.
highlights: Temple of Concordia, Temple of Juno, and Temple of Heracles.

name: Villa Romana del Casale
overview: The center of a large estate upon which the rural economy of the Western Empire was based. It contains the finest mosaics in situ anywhere in the Roman world.
history_significance: Provides exceptional evidence of the Roman social and economic structure in the 4th century.
highlights: The 'Bikini Girls' mosaic and the Great Hunt corridor.

name: Su Nuraxi di Barumini
overview: The finest and most complete example of prehistoric architecture known as the nuraghe, unique to Sardinia. Built of large basalt stones without mortar.
history_significance: Testifies to the innovative use of materials by the Nuragic civilization in the Bronze Age (c. 1500 BC).
highlights: The central defensive tower and the surrounding village.

name: Archaeological Area and the Patriarchal Basilica of Aquileia
overview: Aquileia was one of the largest and wealthiest cities of the Early Roman Empire. Most of it still lies unexcavated beneath the fields. The Patriarchal Basilica is an outstanding building.
history_significance: Played a decisive role in the spread of Christianity into Central Europe. Known for its vast mosaic pavements.
highlights: The Basilica mosaics and the Roman Forum.

name: Historic Centre of Urbino
overview: A small hill town that experienced a great cultural flowering in the 15th century, attracting artists like Raphael and Piero della Francesca.
history_significance: A pinnacle of Renaissance art and architecture, influencing Europe.
highlights: Palazzo Ducale and the birthplace of Raphael.

name: Cilento and Vallo di Diano National Park with the Archaeological Sites of Paestum and Velia, and the Certosa di Padula
overview: A cultural landscape of exceptional quality, linking the classical cities of Paestum and Velia with the medieval Certosa di Padula.
history_significance: A major route for trade and cultural interaction since prehistoric times.
highlights: Greek Temples of Paestum, Velia ruins, and the Certosa di Padula.

name: Villa Adriana (Hadrian's Villa)
overview: The Villa Adriana was an exceptional complex of classical buildings created in the 2nd century A.D. by the Roman emperor Hadrian.
history_significance: Combines the best elements of the architectural heritage of Egypt, Greece, and Rome in the form of an 'ideal city'.
highlights: The Canopus, Maritime Theatre, and the Piazza d'Oro.

name: City of Verona
overview: Founded in the 1st century B.C., Verona flourished under the rule of the Scaliger family in the 13th and 14th centuries.
history_significance: Preserves a remarkable number of monuments from antiquity, the medieval, and Renaissance periods.
highlights: Arena di Verona, Piazza delle Erbe, and Juliet's House.

name: Isole Eolie (Aeolian Islands)
overview: These volcanic islands (Stromboli, Vulcano, Lipari, etc.) provide an outstanding record of volcanic island-building and destruction.
history_significance: The study of these islands has provided the terms 'Vulcanian' and 'Strombolian' to volcanology.
highlights: Stromboli volcano eruptions and the crater of Vulcano.

name: Assisi, the Basilica of San Francesco and Other Franciscan Sites
overview: Assisi is the birthplace of St. Francis. The Basilica of San Francesco is an outstanding example of a new type of architecture that influenced the development of Italian art.
history_significance: A fundamental reference point for the development of Italian and European art and architecture.
highlights: Basilica of San Francesco (Giotto's frescoes) and Santa Chiara.

name: Villa d'Este, Tivoli
overview: The Villa d'Este, with its palace and garden, is one of the most remarkable and comprehensive illustrations of Renaissance culture at its most refined.
history_significance: Its innovative garden design and water features served as a model for gardens across Europe.
highlights: The Fountain of the Organ, the Hundred Fountains, and the Oval Fountain.

name: Late Baroque Towns of the Val di Noto (South-Eastern Sicily)
overview: The eight towns in south-eastern Sicily were all rebuilt in 1693 after a terrible earthquake. They represent a considerable collective undertaking.
history_significance: The highest expression of Late Baroque architecture in Europe.
highlights: Noto Cathedral, Ragusa Ibla, and Modica.

name: Monte San Giorgio
overview: The pyramid-shaped, wooded mountain of Monte San Giorgio beside Lake Lugano is regarded as the best fossil record of marine life from the Triassic Period (245–230 million years ago). It is a transboundary site shared by Switzerland and Italy.
history_significance: The site records life in a tropical lagoon environment, including reptiles, fish, bivalves, ammonites, echinoderms, and crustaceans.
highlights: Fossil Museum in Meride (Switzerland) and the Civic Fossil Museum in Besano (Italy).

locations.name: Fossil Museum of Monte San Giorgio (Meride, Switzerland)

name: Sacri Monti of Piedmont and Lombardy
overview: The nine Sacri Monti (Sacred Mountains) consist of groups of chapels and other architectural features created in the late 16th and 17th centuries.
history_significance: Designed to offer a pilgrimage alternative to the Holy Land, integrating architecture into the natural landscape.
highlights: Sacro Monte of Varallo and Sacro Monte of Orta.

name: Val d'Orcia
overview: The landscape of Val d'Orcia is part of the agricultural hinterland of Siena, redesigned in the 14th and 15th centuries to reflect an idealized model of good governance.
history_significance: An icon of the Renaissance agricultural landscape, often depicted in Sienese paintings.
highlights: Rolling hills with cypress trees, Pienza, and Montalcino.

name: Etruscan Necropolises of Cerveteri and Tarquinia
overview: These two large Etruscan cemeteries reflect different types of burial practices from the 9th to the 1st century BC and bear witness to the achievements of Etruscan culture.
history_significance: Contains thousands of tombs organized like a city plan, with exceptional wall paintings.
highlights: Banditaccia Necropolis (Cerveteri) and Monterozzi Necropolis (Tarquinia).

name: Syracuse and the Rocky Necropolis of Pantalica
overview: The site consists of two separate elements: the Necropolis of Pantalica with over 5,000 tombs, and the ancient city of Syracuse (Ortigia).
history_significance: Syracuse was one of the greatest cities of the ancient Mediterranean, visited by Plato and home to Archimedes.
highlights: Greek Theatre of Syracuse, Ear of Dionysius, and Ortigia Island.

name: Genoa: Le Strade Nuove and the system of the Palazzi dei Rolli
overview: The Strade Nuove (New Streets) built by the Genoese aristocracy in the 16th and 17th centuries are lined with Palazzi dei Rolli, authorized to host state visits.
history_significance: The first example in Europe of an urban development project parcelled out by a public authority.
highlights: Via Garibaldi, Palazzo Rosso, and Palazzo Bianco.

name: Rhaetian Railway in the Albula / Bernina Landscapes
overview: This transboundary property brings together two historic railway lines that cross the Swiss Alps through two passes. The Albula line and the Bernina line (which connects to Italy) constitute an outstanding technical, architectural and environmental ensemble.
history_significance: A masterpiece of engineering from the early 20th century, using viaducts, tunnels, and spiral loops to overcome the isolation of the Alps and vast elevation differences.
highlights: Landwasser Viaduct, Brusio Spiral Viaduct, and the Bernina Pass.

name: Mantua and Sabbioneta
overview: Mantua and Sabbioneta represent two aspects of Renaissance town planning: Mantua shows irregular renewal, while Sabbioneta represents the implementation of the ideal city.
history_significance: Both cities offer exceptional testimonies to the urban, architectural, and artistic realizations of the Renaissance.
highlights: Palazzo Ducale (Mantua) and the Teatro all'Antica (Sabbioneta).

name: The Dolomites
overview: The site comprises a mountain range in the northern Italian Alps, numbering 18 peaks which rise to above 3,000 metres. It features spectacular vertical walls and narrow valleys.
history_significance: Renowned for their geological significance (fossil records) and sheer natural beauty.
highlights: Tre Cime di Lavaredo, Marmolada, and Cinque Torri.

name: Longobards in Italy. Places of the Power (568-774 AD)
overview: This serial property comprises seven groups of important buildings throughout the Italian peninsula, including fortresses, churches, and monasteries.
history_significance: Testifies to the Longobards' major role in the spiritual and cultural development of medieval European Christianity.
highlights: Tempietto Longobardo (Cividale del Friuli) and the San Salvatore complex (Brescia).

name: Medici Villas and Gardens in Tuscany
overview: Twelve villas and two pleasure gardens spread across the Tuscan landscape testify to the influence of the Medici family over modern European culture.
history_significance: Represents the first example of the connection between habitat, gardens, and the environment (Humanist villa).
highlights: Villa di Castello, Villa La Petraia, and Boboli Gardens.

name: Mount Etna
overview: Mount Etna is the highest Mediterranean island mountain and the most active stratovolcano in the world. The site covers 19,237 ha.
history_significance: Provides a continuous record of volcanic eruptions for 2,700 years.
highlights: Summit Craters and the Valle del Bove.

name: Vineyard Landscape of Piedmont: Langhe-Roero and Monferrato
overview: This landscape covers five distinct wine-growing areas with outstanding landscapes and the Castle of Cavour.
history_significance: The birthplace of Barolo and Barbaresco wines, showing a deep knowledge of viticulture.
highlights: Barolo vineyards, Grinzane Cavour Castle, and the Underground Cathedrals of Canelli.

name: Arab-Norman Palermo and the Cathedral Churches of Cefalú and Monreale
overview: Located on the northern coast of Sicily, this serial property includes nine structures dating from the era of the Norman Kingdom of Sicily (1130-1194).
history_significance: An example of social-cultural syncretism between Western, Islamic, and Byzantine cultures.
highlights: Palatine Chapel, Monreale Cathedral (mosaics), and Cefalú Cathedral.

locations.name: Church of San Giovanni degli Eremiti

name: Venetian Works of Defence between the 16th and 17th centuries: Stato da Terra – western Stato da Mar
overview: This transnational serial property consists of 6 components of defence works located in Italy, Croatia, and Montenegro, spanning more than 1,000 km between the Lombard region of Italy and the eastern Adriatic Coast.
history_significance: Illustrates the introduction of the bastioned fort system ('alla moderna') by the Republic of Venice to protect its 'Stato da Terra' (mainland) and 'Stato da Mar' (sea routes).
highlights: The star-shaped city of Palmanova, the defensive system of Zadar, and the Fortified City of Kotor.

name: Ivrea, industrial city of the 20th century
overview: Founded in 1908 by Camillo Olivetti, Ivrea is an industrial city designed as a testing ground for a new vision of the relationship between industrial production and social architecture.
history_significance: A model social project of the Olivetti company, expressing a modern vision of the relationship between production and architecture.
highlights: The Olivetti factory buildings and the residential units.

name: The Prosecco Hills of Conegliano and Valdobbiadene
overview: Located in north-eastern Italy, the site includes part of the vine-growing landscape of the Prosecco wine production area. It is characterized by distinct 'hogback' hills.
history_significance: Famous for the 'ciglioni' (grassy terraces) and the checkerboard landscape of vineyards.
highlights: The steep vineyards and the panoramic views of the hills.

name: Padua's fourteenth-century fresco cycles
overview: The site comprises eight religious and secular building complexes within the historic walled city of Padua, housing fresco cycles painted between 1302 and 1397.
history_significance: Includes Giotto's Scrovegni Chapel, which revolutionized mural painting with its new perspective and depiction of human emotion.
highlights: Scrovegni Chapel, Palazzo della Ragione, and Basilica of Saint Anthony.

name: The Porticoes of Bologna
overview: The serial property comprises twelve component parts consisting of ensembles of porticoes and their surrounding built areas, located within the Municipality of Bologna.
history_significance: An outstanding architectural heritage stretching over 62 km, serving as sheltered walkways and commercial spaces since the Middle Ages.
highlights: Portico of San Luca (longest in the world) and the Pavaglione Portico.

name: Evaporitic Karst and Caves of the Northern Apennines
overview: This serial property is an unusually well-preserved and extensive epigenic gypsum karst terrain. It includes a very high density of caves.
history_significance: Contains over 900 caves with a total of over 100 km of tunnels, offering a complete record of karst evolution.
highlights: Gessi Bolognesi and the Cave of Spipola.

name: Via Appia. Regina Viarum
overview: The Via Appia (Appian Way) was the first and most important of the great roads built by the Ancient Romans, known as the 'Queen of Roads'.
history_significance: A feat of Roman engineering begun in 312 BC, connecting Rome to Brindisi and facilitating military and economic expansion.
highlights: Mausoleum of Cecilia Metella, Villa of the Quintilii, and the ancient paving stones.

name: Funerary Tradition in the Prehistory of Sardinia – The domus de janas
overview: A serial property comprising 'Domus de Janas' (House of the Fairies) rock-cut tombs. These prehistoric burial structures reflect the beliefs and rituals of Sardinian communities from the Neolithic to the Bronze Age.
history_significance: Represents a unique and elaborate funerary architecture with decorative motifs symbolizing regeneration.
highlights: Necropolis of Anghelu Ruju and Sant'Andrea Priu.

name: The Great Wall
overview: Built over 2,000 years, from the 3rd century B.C. to the Ming Dynasty (1368–1644), to protect Chinese states and empires against the raids and invasions of the various nomadic groups of the Eurasian Steppe.
history_significance: The largest man-made military structure in the world, stretching over 20,000 km, demonstrating military strategy and defense integration.
highlights: Badaling, Mutianyu, and Simatai sections.

name: Mausoleum of the First Qin Emperor
overview: The mausoleum of Qin Shi Huang, the first emperor of unified China (221–210 BC), contains some 6,000 life-size terracotta figures of soldiers and horses.
history_significance: A matchless work of art and a key reference for the study of the unification of China.
highlights: The Terracotta Army (Bingmayong) and the tomb mound.

name: Mogao Caves
overview: Located on the Silk Road, Mogao is the best-known of the Buddhist cave temples of China. It contains 492 cells and cave sanctuaries decorated with wall paintings and stucco statues.
history_significance: A thousand years of Buddhist art (4th to 14th centuries) and cultural exchange along the Silk Road.
highlights: The Library Cave and the 9-storey Building (Giant Buddha).

name: Imperial Palaces of the Ming and Qing Dynasties in Beijing and Shenyang
overview: The Forbidden City in Beijing (built in the 15th century) and the Imperial Palace in Shenyang (17th century) were the centers of supreme power for over five centuries.
history_significance: Outstanding examples of Chinese imperial architecture and residential complexes, representing the culmination of the tradition.
highlights: The Hall of Supreme Harmony (Beijing) and the Dazheng Hall (Shenyang).

name: Peking Man Site at Zhoukoudian
overview: The site contains the remains of 'Peking Man', a Homo erectus, who lived in the Middle Pleistocene, as well as Homo sapiens remains from the Late Palaeolithic.
history_significance: A key site demonstrating human evolution in Asia.
highlights: Cave 1 (Ape-man Cave) and the Upper Cave.

name: Mount Taishan
overview: Mount Taishan is considered one of the five sacred mountains of China. It has been the site of imperial ceremonies and worship for 2,000 years.
history_significance: Symbolizes the ancient Chinese civilization and belief system, linking culture with the natural landscape.
highlights: The Taishan Summit and the Dai Temple.

name: Mount Huangshan
overview: Known for its magnificent scenery, Mount Huangshan features granite peaks emerging from the clouds and oddly-shaped pine trees.
history_significance: A major site for artistic and literary inspiration (the 'Huangshan school' of landscape painting and Shanshui literature).
highlights: The Sea of Clouds, peculiar rock formations, and the Guest-Greeting Pine.

name: Jiuzhaigou Valley Scenic and Historic Interest Area
overview: Jiuzhaigou contains a series of diverse forest ecosystems, spectacular waterfalls, and narrow conic karst landforms.
history_significance: Renowned for its crystal-clear, colorful lakes reflecting the surrounding snow-capped peaks.
highlights: Five Flower Lake and Nuorilang Waterfall.

name: Huanglong Scenic and Historic Interest Area
overview: Huanglong is famed for its colorful pools formed by calcite deposits, as well as diverse forest ecosystems and snow-capped peaks.
history_significance: Home to the endangered Giant Panda and Sichuan Golden Snub-nosed Monkey.
highlights: The Five-Colored Pond and the Yellow Dragon Temple.

name: Wulingyuan Scenic and Historic Interest Area
overview: The site is dominated by more than 3,000 narrow sandstone pillars and peaks, many over 200m high.
history_significance: A spectacular landscape that inspired the floating mountains in the movie 'Avatar'.
highlights: Zhangjiajie National Forest Park and Tianzi Mountain.

name: Historic Ensemble of the Potala Palace, Lhasa
overview: The Potala Palace, winter palace of the Dalai Lama from the 7th century, symbolizes Tibetan Buddhism and its central role in the traditional administration of Tibet.
history_significance: A massive architectural complex including the White Palace (administrative) and the Red Palace (religious).
highlights: The Red and White Palaces and the golden roofs.

name: Mountain Resort and its Outlying Temples, Chengde
overview: The Mountain Resort was the summer palace of the Qing dynasty emperors. It is a vast complex of palaces and gardens.
history_significance: Surrounded by Eight Outer Temples built in various architectural styles (Tibetan, Han, Mongolian) to symbolize national unity.
highlights: The Putuo Zongcheng Temple (Little Potala) and the Imperial Summer Resort.

name: Temple and Cemetery of Confucius and the Kong Family Mansion in Qufu
overview: The complex of monuments at Qufu has retained its outstanding artistic and historic character due to the devotion of successive Chinese emperors to Confucius.
history_significance: The birthplace of Confucianism and a major pilgrimage site for over 2,000 years.
highlights: Temple of Confucius, Cemetery of Confucius, and the Kong Family Mansion.

name: Ancient Building Complex in the Wudang Mountains
overview: The palaces and temples of the Wudang Mountains constitute a complex of Taoist buildings from the Yuan, Ming, and Qing dynasties.
history_significance: A center of Taoism and martial arts, exemplifying Chinese architectural and artistic achievements.
highlights: Golden Hall and Nanyan Temple.

name: Lushan National Park
overview: Mount Lushan is one of the spiritual centers of Chinese civilization. Buddhist and Taoist temples, along with landmarks of Confucianism, blend into the landscape.
history_significance: Combines natural beauty with cultural history, including historic villas from the early 20th century.
highlights: White Deer Grotto Academy and the Meilu Villa.

name: Mount Emei Scenic Area, including Leshan Giant Buddha Scenic Area
overview: Mount Emei is the birthplace of Buddhism in China. The colossal Leshan Giant Buddha (71m high) was carved out of a hillside in the 8th century.
history_significance: An area of great cultural significance, containing a remarkable diversity of vegetation and a tradition of temple building.
highlights: The Leshan Giant Buddha and the Golden Summit of Mount Emei.

name: Ancient City of Ping Yao
overview: Ping Yao is an exceptionally well-preserved example of a traditional Han Chinese city. It was a major financial center during the Qing dynasty.
history_significance: Provides a complete picture of the Chinese cultural, social, economic, and religious development in the 14th to 19th centuries.
highlights: The Ancient City Wall, Rishengchang Exchange Shop, and the Temple of the City God.

name: Classical Gardens of Suzhou
overview: Suzhou is famous for its classical gardens, which recreate natural landscapes in miniature. They are masterpieces of Chinese landscape garden design.
history_significance: Reflects the profound metaphysical importance of natural beauty in Chinese culture.
highlights: The Humble Administrator's Garden, The Lingering Garden, and the Master of the Nets Garden.

name: Old Town of Lijiang
overview: The Old Town of Lijiang is located in a spectacular landscape. It has a unique architectural style blending elements from several cultures (Naxi, Han, Tibetan).
history_significance: An ancient trading center on the Tea Horse Road, known for its complex water supply system.
highlights: Dayan Old Town, Black Dragon Pool, and the Mu Palace.

name: Summer Palace, an Imperial Garden in Beijing
overview: The Summer Palace (Yiheyuan) is a masterpiece of Chinese landscape garden design, dominated by Longevity Hill and Kunming Lake.
history_significance: Combines political and administrative areas with residential and recreational quarters.
highlights: The Long Corridor, Kunming Lake, and the Tower of Buddhist Incense.

name: Temple of Heaven: an Imperial Sacrificial Altar in Beijing
overview: The Temple of Heaven (Tiantan) was where emperors of the Ming and Qing dynasties performed ceremonies of worship to heaven to pray for good harvests.
history_significance: A masterpiece of architecture and landscape design which simply and graphically illustrates a cosmogony of great importance.
highlights: The Hall of Prayer for Good Harvests and the Echo Wall.

name: Dazu Rock Carvings
overview: The steep hillsides of the Dazu area contain an exceptional series of rock carvings dating from the 9th to the 13th century.
history_significance: Remarkable for their aesthetic quality and their harmony of Buddhism, Taoism, and Confucianism.
highlights: Baodingshan Carvings (Thousand-Armed Avalokitesvara) and Beishan Carvings.

name: Mount Wuyi
overview: Mount Wuyi is known for its spectacular riverine landscape and as the cradle of Neo-Confucianism.
history_significance: The area contains the remains of the ancient Han City and numerous temples.
highlights: Nine Bend River (Jiuquxi) rafting and the Heavenly Tour Peak.

name: Mount Qingcheng and the Dujiangyan Irrigation System
overview: Dujiangyan is an irrigation system built in 256 BC that is still in use today. Mount Qingcheng is the birthplace of Taoism.
history_significance: Dujiangyan controls the waters of the Minjiang River without the use of dams, a feat of ancient engineering.
highlights: Fish Mouth Levee and the Taoist temples of Qingcheng.

name: Ancient Villages in Southern Anhui – Xidi and Hongcun
overview: The two traditional villages of Xidi and Hongcun preserve the appearance of non-urban settlements of a type that largely disappeared or was transformed during the last century.
history_significance: Famous for their 'Hui' style architecture, water systems, and decorative carvings.
highlights: Moon Pond (Hongcun) and the intricate wood carvings.

name: Longmen Grottoes
overview: The Longmen Grottoes contain the largest and most impressive collection of Chinese art of the late Northern Wei and Tang Dynasties.
history_significance: More than 2,300 caves and niches carved into the limestone cliffs of the Yi River.
highlights: Fengxian Temple (Vairocana Buddha) and Guyang Cave.

name: Imperial Tombs of the Ming and Qing Dynasties
overview: This site includes the Ming Tombs near Beijing, the Qing Tombs in Hebei, and the Ming Xiaoling Tomb in Nanjing.
history_significance: Illustrates the geomantic principles (Feng Shui) and architectural traditions of imperial burials.
highlights: The Sacred Way (Ming Tombs) and the Dingling Tomb (underground palace).

name: Yungang Grottoes
overview: The Yungang Grottoes represent an outstanding achievement of Buddhist cave art in China during the 5th and 6th centuries.
history_significance: The Five Caves created by Tan Yao are masterpieces of the first peak of Chinese Buddhist art.
highlights: Cave 20 (The Colossal Buddha).

name: Three Parallel Rivers of Yunnan Protected Areas
overview: The site consists of eight geographical clusters where the Yangtze, Mekong, and Salween rivers run roughly parallel.
history_significance: One of the richest temperate regions of the world in terms of biodiversity.
highlights: Meili Snow Mountain, Tiger Leaping Gorge, and the Kawagebo Peak.

name: Capital Cities and Tombs of the Ancient Koguryo Kingdom
overview: The site includes archaeological remains of three cities and 40 tombs of the Koguryo Kingdom (37 BC – AD 668).
history_significance: Testifies to the early history of the Koguryo dynasty, which ruled over parts of northern China and the northern Korean Peninsula.
highlights: Gwanggaeto Stele, Tomb of the General (Janggun-chong), and Wandu Mountain City.

name: Historic Centre of Macao
overview: Macao was a strategic port that played a pivotal role in early international trade between East and West. It reflects a unique blend of Chinese and Portuguese cultural, architectural, and religious traditions.
history_significance: The site represents the oldest European architectural heritage in China and stands as an exceptional testimony to cultural exchange over more than 400 years.
highlights: Ruins of St. Paul's, Senado Square, A-Ma Temple, Guia Fortress.

locations.name: Sam Kai Vui Kun (Kuan Tai Temple)
Cathedral of the Nativity of Our Lady
Section of the Old City Walls

name: Yin Xu
overview: Yin Xu was the last capital of the Shang Dynasty (1300–1046 BC). It is famous for the discovery of oracle bones and the tomb of Fu Hao.
history_significance: The archaeological site provides evidence of the early Bronze Age civilization in China and the earliest known Chinese writing.
highlights: Tomb of Fu Hao and the Oracle Bone Pit.

name: Sichuan Giant Panda Sanctuaries - Wolong, Mt Siguniang and Jiajin Mountains
overview: Home to more than 30% of the world's giant pandas. The sanctuaries cover 924,500 ha.
history_significance: One of the botanically richest sites of any temperate region in the world.
highlights: Wolong Nature Reserve and Mount Siguniang.

name: Kaiping Diaolou and Villages
overview: The Diaolou are multi-storied defensive village houses built by overseas Chinese, displaying a complex blending of Chinese and Western architectural forms.
history_significance: Reflects the significant role of émigré Kaiping people in the development of the region in the late 19th and early 20th centuries.
highlights: Zili Village and the Li Garden.

name: South China Karst
overview: A serial site spanning the provinces of Guizhou, Guangxi, Yunnan, and Chongqing, representing one of the world's most spectacular examples of humid tropical to subtropical karst landscapes.
history_significance: Famous for the Stone Forest (Shilin) and the fenglin/fengcong (tower/cone) karst formations.
highlights: Shilin Stone Forest, Libo Karst, and Wulong Karst.

name: Fujian Tulou
overview: The Tulou are the unique earthen castles of the Hakka people in southwest Fujian. They are large, enclosed, and fortified earth buildings, mostly circular or rectangular.
history_significance: Exceptional examples of a building tradition and function exemplifying a particular type of communal living.
highlights: Tianluokeng Tulou Cluster and Chengqilou (King of Tulou).

name: Mount Sanqingshan National Park
overview: Mount Sanqingshan features a concentration of fantastically shaped granite pillars and peaks.
history_significance: A masterpiece of nature, often surrounded by mist and clouds.
highlights: The Oriental Goddess and the Python Coming out of the Mountain.

name: Mount Wutai
overview: Mount Wutai is a sacred Buddhist mountain, home to 53 sacred monasteries. It is the global center of Manjusri worship.
history_significance: Features the East Main Hall of Foguang Temple, the highest surviving timber building of the Tang Dynasty.
highlights: Foguang Temple and Tayuan Temple.

name: Historic Monuments of Dengfeng in 'The Centre of Heaven and Earth'
